subject: english
topic_title: Speech Work: More on Consonant Sounds – Spelling Variants
learning_objectives: Identify the spelling variants for /b/, /p/, /f/, /g/, /dʒ/, and /h/
Give word examples for each spelling variant

sections.heading: /b/ – Voiced Bilabial Plosive
sections.content:
'b' spellings: baby, bank, break, barber, lab, embark
'bb' spellings: rubber, robber, babble

sections.heading: /p/ – Voiceless Bilabial Plosive
sections.content:
'p' spellings: pain, people, leap, leper, emperor
'gh' spellings: hiccough

sections.heading: /f/ – Voiceless Labio-Dental Fricative
sections.content:
'f' spellings: form, frog, faith, fruit, proof, soft
'ff' spellings: cliff, stuff, sniff, cuff, huff
'gh' spellings: rough, tough, cough, enough
'ph' spellings: physics, physical, Philip, phantom
'v' spellings: Gorbachev, Volkswagen

sections.heading: /g/ – Voiced Velar Plosive
sections.content:
'g' spellings: goat, gun, bag, vogue, vigour, garnish, goose, gamble, group
'gg' spellings: haggle, baggage, baggy, struggle, bigger, shaggy
'gh' spellings: ghost, aghast, ghetto, ghoul

sections.heading: /dʒ/ – Voiced Post-Alveolar Affricate
sections.content:
'j' spellings: jug, junior, Jew
'ge' spellings: germinate, damage, badge, germ
'gi' spellings: ginger, giant, giraffe, gigantic
'gy' spellings: technology, archaeology, gym
'du' spellings: educate, graduate, gradual, due

sections.heading: /h/ – Voiceless Glottal Fricative
sections.content:
'h' spellings: hope, horse, holy, perhaps, hunger
'wh' spellings: whole, wholly, who, whose
